sections.paragraph:
pr. to move the feet, to go, to tread (denom. from רֶגֶל, cogn. to רָכַל), specially-

(1) to go about, to calumniate and slander; hence to calumniate, Psa 15:3.

(2) to tread upon garments which are to be washed and cleansed from dirt; hence רֹגֵל a washer, a fuller. See the pr.n. עֵין רֹגֵל and רֹגְלִים.

Piel, i.q. Kal, to go about-(a) to calumniate, 2Sa 19:28 followed by בְּ of pers.-(b) in order to explore; to explore; followed by an acc. Jos 14:7 Jud. 18:2 , 14 Jdg 18:14, 17 Jdg 18:17 2Sa 10:3, etc. Part. מְרַגֵּל a spy, an explorer, Gen 42:9, seq.; Jos 6:22.

Tiphel תִּרְגֵּל i.q. Syr. ܫܪܰܓܶܠ to teach to go, to lead (children) by the hand, Hos 11:3.
